L1.area: Runtime extension module operation registration and dispatch

L2.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists in the runtime where certain low-level operations for interacting with native code and memory fail to enforce required permission and stability checks. A malicious actor controlling code within the sandboxed execution environment can bypass the permission model by directly invoking these ungated operations, enabling arbitrary code execution even when the necessary dangerous capabilities have not been explicitly granted by the user.

L3.area: Runtime extension module operation registration and dispatch
L3.description: A sandbox escape vulnerability exists in the runtime where certain low-level operations for interacting with native code and memory fail to enforce required permission and stability checks. A malicious actor controlling code within the sandboxed execution environment can bypass the permission model by directly invoking these ungated operations, enabling arbitrary code execution even when the necessary dangerous capabilities have not been explicitly granted by the user.

ground_truth.reason: Certain FFI operations for calling native function pointers did not enforce permission or stability checks, allowing code within the sandbox to bypass the runtime's permission model and execute arbitrary native code.
